Off Calendar Dec-13-2007 Off Calendar

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Dec-13-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. LEVON SAGATELYAN APPEARING FOR ANNE SCHNELL AS EXECUTRIX OF THE PETER SCHNELL ESTATE;
ATTY. CHARLES PAUL WOLFF FOR THE PETITIONER EUGENE A. SCHNELL JR.

THE COURT INFORMED COUNSELS THAT SHE DOES NOT SEE A COPY OF THE RUTH SCHNELL GRAHAM TRUST IN THE FILE. THE COURT QUESTIONED COUNSEL IF THERE IS ANY REMAINDER INTEREST IF PETER SCHNELL DIES. ATTY SAGATELYAN CONFIRMED THAT IT GOES OUTRIGHT TO BOTH PETER AND EUGENE. AFTER REVIEWING THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT, THE COURT APPROVED THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. THE HEARING ON THE DEMURRER WAS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR.

ATTY SAGATELYAN STATED THAT HE WILL FILE ANOTHER MOTION APPROVING SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT WITH COOPER, WHITE & COOPER AND KENNETH GOODIN. THE COURT AGREED THAT COUNSEL MAY SUBMIT IT AS AN EXPEDITED EX-PARTE PETITION.

COMMISSIONER: DOROTHY MCMATH, JUDGE PRO-TEM; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN MENDOZA

Motion To Dismiss With Prejudice First Amended Petition And All Claims And This Actio As To Respondents Cooper, White & Cooper Llp And Kenneth E. Goodin; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-27-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Richard Canatella appearing for petitioner Peter R. Schnell. Ken Philpot appearing for Cooper, White Cooper and Ken Goodin. Charles Wolff appearing for Eugene Schnell.
Counsel have seen the tentative ruling. Mr. Wolff stated he is here to observe only. Oral arguments presented by Mr. Canatella and by Mr. Philpot. Court did strike all causes of action as to apply to Cooper, White Cooper and Ken Goodin. Court's tentative ruling will stand. Mr. Canatella requested for five days to review the order. Court will hold the order to give Mr. Canatella a chance to review the order.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Handa.

Supplemental Verified Petition For: 1. Report Of Co-Trustee; 2. Request For Accounting...
Off Calendar Jun-04-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Richard Canatella appearing for petitioner Peter Schnell. Ken Philpot appearing for Cooper, White & Cooper and Ken Goodin. Naznin Challa appearing for Eugene Schnell. Tentative Ruling on Motion to strike suplemental petition and reconsidering and vacating order sustaining demurrer are denied. There are no authority to strike their own pleading. Motion to file amended petition is granted with causes of action against Cooper White Cooper and Goodin striken. There is no formal motion to dismiss Cooper White Cooper today. Oral argument presented by Mr. Canatella. Mr. Philpot argued that a Motion to dismiss is not necessary and he will submit a follow up brief in support of his position. Ms. Challa asked for clarification on answering the Amended petition. Tentative ruling stands.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Mary Ellen Scherer, CSR# 5208. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: M. Loos.

Respondents Cooper, White & Cooper'S Llp'S And K. E. Goofin'S Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petitioner'S Verified Petition.
Off Calendar Apr-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Richard Canatella appearing for petitioner Peter Schnell. Kenneth Philpot appearing for Cooper, White & Cooper and Ken Goodin. Charles Wolff and Naznin Challa appearing for Eugene Schell, Jr.. Counsel have seen the court's tentative ruling. Mr. Canatella objected to the Court striking the amended petition. The Court's ruling does not strike the amended petition. Court has not been asked to strike it. The petition filed on 9/19/06 is superseded by the supplemental petition filed on 2/6/07. This demurrer is to the supplemental petition. The first amended petition filed on 4/3/07 is set for hearing on 5/15. The issue on is the first amended petition filed properly is not before the court today. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the three causes of action in the supplemental petition. Order signed. The hearing on the supplemental petition is continued to 6/4. The hearing on the amended petition on 5/15 is continued to 6/4 by stipulation of counsel. Court is not making a finding in any way the amended petition is filed property.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Teena Paxton CSR# 1558. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: D. Handa.
